commonMistakes.feedback: Your alif needs to be taller — draw it from the top all the way down.
Start your alif at the top and come down — not from the bottom up.
Alif is a straight line — try to keep it as straight as you can.
Keep alif straight and tall — one clean line down. Try again.
Make it taller — alif reaches from the top line to the bottom.
A little straighter — alif stands up tall, it doesn't lean.
Keep alif straight and tall — one clean line down. Try again.
Make it taller — alif reaches from the top line to the bottom.
A little straighter — alif stands up tall, it doesn't lean.
Baa is two parts — the boat, then one dot underneath.
Draw the boat first, then the dot underneath.
Baa's dot goes under the boat, not on top.
That looks like a different letter — try baa again.

letters._contextualFormsNote: DRAFT per-positional centerlines (open pen-tip paths, normalized 0..1) for the four baa forms. The isolated form ports the validated boat+dot; initial/medial/final are best-effort drafts and NEED a real-tablet re-capture via /dev/authoring at the owner's-mother sign-off (the per-form pen motion is HER call). Every form stays signedOff:false until she reviews. The dot is always below; body sweeps rightToLeft.

commonMistakes.feedback: Your baa needs a deeper curve at the bottom — try again, slower this time.
The bowl is good — now place one dot just below it.
Baa's dot goes under the boat, not on top. That dot up there makes a different letter.
Draw the boat first, then add the dot underneath.
At the start of a word, baa has no tail — keep it small and flat so it can reach the next letter.
A little flatter here — the starting baa is a low hump, not a full bowl. Try again.
Good shape — now add the one dot just below it.
The dot belongs underneath baa, not above. Move it down and try again.
Smaller — in the middle, baa is just a little tooth between two letters. No bowl, no tail.
Keep your pen down — the medial baa joins on both sides. Don't lift between the letters.
The little tooth is right — now its one dot goes just below.
Baa keeps its dot underneath, even in the middle of a word. Move it down.
At the end of a word, let baa's tail return into the full bowl — give it that deep sweep.
Deeper curve at the bottom — the final baa rests in a full bowl. Try again, slower.
Lovely bowl — now place the one dot just below it.
The dot stays underneath baa at the end too — move it below the bowl.
Taa is the boat plus two dots on top — count them: one, two.
Draw the boat first, then the two dots on top.
Taa's two dots go above the boat, not below.
That looks like a different letter — try taa again.
Your taa needs a deeper boat at the bottom — try again, slower.
Taa has TWO dots, not one — add the second dot on top.
Taa's two dots go ABOVE the boat, not below. Move them up.
Draw the boat first, then add the two dots on top.
Your taa needs a deeper boat at the bottom — try again, slower.
Taa has TWO dots, not one — add the second dot on top.
Taa's two dots go ABOVE the boat, not below. Move them up.
Draw the boat first, then add the two dots on top.
Your taa needs a deeper boat at the bottom — try again, slower.
Taa has TWO dots, not one — add the second dot on top.
Taa's two dots go ABOVE the boat, not below. Move them up.
Draw the boat first, then add the two dots on top.
Your taa needs a deeper boat at the bottom — try again, slower.
Taa has TWO dots, not one — add the second dot on top.
Taa's two dots go ABOVE the boat, not below. Move them up.
Draw the boat first, then add the two dots on top.
Thaa is the boat plus three dots on top — count them: one, two, three.
Draw the boat first, then the three dots.
Thaa has three dots above the boat, like a little triangle.
That looks like a different letter — try thaa again.
Jeem is the curl, then one dot inside underneath.
Draw the curl first, then the dot.
Jeem's dot sits inside, below the curve — not on top.
That looks like a different letter — try jeem again.
Haa is one smooth curl with a round belly — no dot at all.
Start at the top and sweep down into the belly.
Give the belly room — let the curve drop down.
That looks like a different letter — try haa again.
Khaa is the curl, then one dot on top.
Draw the curl first, then the dot above.
Khaa's one dot goes above the curve.
That looks like a different letter — try khaa again.
Daal is a little open hook — keep the corner, no full curve.
Start at the top and come down into the hook.
Make the hook a bit bigger.
That looks like a different letter — try daal again.
Dhaal is the hook, then one dot on top.
Draw the hook first, then the dot above.
Dhaal's one dot goes above the hook.
That looks like a different letter — try dhaal again.
Raa is a smooth curve that dips down below the line.
Start at the top and curve down to the left.
Let raa swing down a little lower.
That looks like a different letter — try raa again.
Zaay is raa with one dot on top.
Draw the curve first, then the dot above.
Zaay's one dot goes above the curve.
That looks like a different letter — try zaay again.
Seen has three little teeth, then a bowl — keep all three teeth.
Start on the right and go across to the left.
Make the teeth clear and even.
That looks like a different letter — try seen again.
Sheen is seen with three dots on top — count them: one, two, three.
Draw the teeth first, then the three dots.
Sheen's three dots sit above the teeth, like a little triangle.
That looks like a different letter — try sheen again.
Saad is a round body, then a flat line along the bottom.
Start on the right and work to the left.
Give the body a nice round shape.
That looks like a different letter — try saad again.
Daad is saad with one dot on top.
Draw the body first, then the dot above.
Daad's one dot goes above the body.
That looks like a different letter — try daad again.
Taa is the body and the standing line up top — two parts.
Draw the body first, then the standing line.
Keep the body round and the line straight and tall.
That looks like a different letter — try taa again.
Zhaa is taa plus one dot on top — body, line, then dot.
Body first, then the standing line, then the dot.
Zhaa's dot goes on top, near the line.
That looks like a different letter — try zhaa again.
Ayn opens at the top, then curves down into a belly.
Start at the open top and sweep down.
Let the belly drop down nicely.
That looks like a different letter — try ayn again.
Ghayn is ayn with one dot on top.
Draw the body first, then the dot above.
Ghayn's one dot goes above the head.
That looks like a different letter — try ghayn again.
Faa is the round head and arm, then one dot on top.
Draw the head and arm first, then the dot.
Faa's one dot goes above the head.
That looks like a different letter — try faa again.
Qaaf is the head and tail, then two dots on top — count them: one, two.
Draw the body first, then the two dots.
Qaaf's two dots go above the head.
That looks like a different letter — try qaaf again.
Kaaf is the tall shape and the little mark inside — two parts.
Draw the big shape first, then the little mark inside.
Keep the tall corner of kaaf, with the small mark tucked inside.
That looks like a different letter — try kaaf again.
Laam goes straight down, then hooks along the bottom.
Start at the top and come all the way down before the hook.
Laam is tall — bring it down from the top.
That looks like a different letter — try laam again.
Meem is a little round head, then a tail straight down.
Make the round head first, then drop the tail down.
Let the tail hang down below the head.
That looks like a different letter — try meem again.
Noon is the deep bowl, then one dot on top.
Draw the bowl first, then the dot above.
Noon's one dot goes above the middle of the bowl.
That looks like a different letter — try noon again.
Haa (ه) is a round loop — keep it round and closed-looking.
Start at the top and curl around.
Give the loop a nice round size.
That looks like a different letter — try haa again.
Waaw is a round head with a tail that drops down.
Make the round head first, then the tail down.
Let the tail swing down below the line.
That looks like a different letter — try waaw again.
Yaa is the body with two dots underneath — count them: one, two.
Draw the body first, then the two dots below.
Yaa's two dots go below the body, not above.
That looks like a different letter — try yaa again.
